title: Negative refcount (PyStackRef_XCLOSE/Py_DECREF) in _PyFrame_ClearLocals (Python/frame.c) tearing down a frame during exception unwinding under MemoryError
description: Eval-loop exit_unwind clears a frame's operand stack via _PyFrame_ClearLocals; under OOM an opcode error path left a stale/already-dead _PyStackRef on the value stack, so PyStackRef_XCLOSE->Py_DECREF underflows the object's refcount (debug: negative-refcount abort; release: use-after-free segv). Over-decref'd object is a MemoryError.
notes: Largest group; does NOT split -- 8 vehicles share top frames #11-#15 (PyStackRef_XCLOSE@stackref.h:726 -> _PyFrame_ClearLocals@frame.c:101 -> _PyFrame_ClearExceptCode -> clear_thread_frame -> _PyEval_EvalFrameDefault@generated_cases.c.h:13908 exit_unwind). concurrent_futures_process labeled 'segfault' is CONFIRMED here: abort on ft_debug_asan, segv 5/5 on ft_release+upstream (release = assert compiled out -> use-after-free). jit aborts at a SIBLING assert in the same path (generated_cases.c.h:13817 _PyErr_Occurred). Minimization DONE (2026-06-19): deterministic 12-line stdlib repro `xml.dom.minidom.parse(0)` under the set_nomemory sweep range(100), 20/20 GIL=1 + 8/8 GIL=0 on ft_debug_asan. shrinkray on the xml_dom_minidom vehicle kept the fuzzer weird_classes setup (the final call's arg referenced it); substituting a trivial arg (0) freed it for removal -- the bare parse(0) sweep reproduces alone. The xml.dom.minidom path does NOT trip the sibling code_dealloc assert (Objects/codeobject.c:2440 _co_unique_id) the older multiprocessing.spawn/runpy reduction hit (that sibling is still a candidate for a separate id). dedupe.py = NEW.
